title: The Titans of Nasdaq 100: Top companies ranked by market cap - Nasdaq 100 Titans | The Economic Times
text:
The Nasdaq-100 index represents some of the world’s most innovative and influential companies — spanning artificial intelligence, cloud computing, consumer technology, digital advertising, electric vehicles, and other areas driving global growth. By market capitalization, its top 10 constituents represent a substantial portion of the index’s overall value, highlighting the businesses that continue to shape global markets and everyday life.Explore the largest Nasdaq-100 companies by market capitalization and compare their scale using Trailing Twelve Months (TTM) revenue — the total revenue generated over the latest four reported quarters.

With a market value approaching USD 5 trillion, NVIDIA has emerged as the face of the artificial intelligence revolution.Its powerful semiconductor platforms have become the backbone of AI infrastructure worldwide, driving demand from cloud providers, enterprises, and research institutions.Market Cap: USD 4.91T | TTM Revenue: USD 253.49B

Apple continues to command one of the world’s strongest brands, combining hardware, software, and services into a highly profitable ecosystem. Its massive installed customer base and recurring services revenue keep it among the most valuable companies globally.Market Cap: USD 4.90T | TTM Revenue: USD 451.44B

Alphabet remains a global leader in online search, digital advertising, cloud computing, and artificial intelligence. Through Google’s ecosystem and AI investments, the company continues to influence how information is accessed and consumed worldwide.Market Cap: USD 4.23T | TTM Revenue: USD 422.50B

Microsoft’s transformation into a cloud and AI leader has strengthened its position as one of the world’s most valuable companies. Its platforms power businesses, developers, and organizations across the globe.Market Cap: USD 2.93T | TTM Revenue: USD 318.27B

Amazon stands apart with unmatched revenue scale, powered by e-commerce, cloud computing through AWS, advertising, and logistics. While its market capitalization trails some technology peers, its business footprint remains enormous.Market Cap: USD 2.66T | TTM Revenue: USD 742.78B

Broadcom has become a critical player in networking, semiconductor solutions, and infrastructure technology supporting the digital economy.Market Cap: USD 1.76T | TTM Revenue: USD 75.47B

Meta continues to monetize its massive social media ecosystem while investing heavily in artificial intelligence and next-generation computing platforms.Market Cap: USD 1.64T | TTM Revenue: USD 214.96B

SpaceX represents a new era of commercial space innovation, with ambitious projects spanning satellite connectivity, launch services, and space exploration.Market Cap: USD 1.63T | TTM Revenue: USD 19.30B

Tesla remains one of the most recognized names in electric vehicles, energy storage, and autonomous driving technology. Its valuation reflects expectations around future growth and innovation.Market Cap: USD 1.43T | TTM Revenue: USD 97.88B
